disclaimer: Curated sources for product policy and research traceability; not legal, tax, procurement, or engineering advice.

sources.title: GAEB-Datenaustausch-FAQ
sources.operationalUse: Distinguish X83/X84 content phases and GAEB content exchange from AVA transport integration.
sources.limitations: Does not prove that a particular AVA implementation is correct or provide a universal cloud API.

sources.title: GAEB-Datenaustausch Downloads
sources.operationalUse: Pin the current stable 3.3 package, exact version/date, and official XSDs; keep 3.4 beta blocked.
sources.limitations: Current-version status can change and must be rechecked before a production release.

sources.title: Fachdokumentation GAEB DA XML 3.3
sources.operationalUse: Design deterministic X83/X84 structure mapping and version validation.
sources.limitations: Formal validity does not prove technical correctness, commercial completeness, authority, or contract formation.

sources.title: GWB §97 — Principles of award procedures
sources.operationalUse: Hard-stop public or unclear procurement for specialist review.
sources.limitations: Must be read with contracting-authority, threshold, state, budget, grant, and flow-down rules.

sources.title: GWB §99 — Public contracting authorities
sources.operationalUse: Do not infer private procurement merely from private legal form; capture public funding and project purpose.
sources.limitations: Application is fact-specific and requires legal/procurement review.

sources.title: StGB §201 — Confidentiality of the spoken word
sources.operationalUse: Recording off until affirmative informed permission; restrict subsequent use/access.
sources.limitations: Exact treatment of all transient processing patterns requires counsel.

sources.title: DSK decision on recording telephone calls
sources.operationalUse: Require explicit opt-in before recording; silence/continuation is not consent.
sources.limitations: Guidance does not select the lawful basis for every downstream processing purpose.

sources.title: EU AI Act Article 50 and Article 113
sources.operationalUse: Disclose AI no later than first interaction; implement before Article 50 applies on 2026-08-02.
sources.limitations: Does not establish a final product-risk classification or mean every AI Act provision has the same application date.

sources.title: General Data Protection Regulation
sources.operationalUse: Design purpose, lawful basis, notice, minimization, processor, security, retention, rights, DPIA, and transfer controls.
sources.limitations: No single legal basis or DPIA conclusion can be hardcoded for every customer and data flow.

sources.title: BGB §145 — Binding effect of an offer
sources.operationalUse: Prohibit agent wording that accepts, awards, orders, or declares a final agreement.
sources.limitations: Contract formation depends on all declarations, authority, form, reservations, and facts; a phone call is not automatically nonbinding.

sources.title: BGB §§164 and 177 — Representation and authority
sources.operationalUse: Separate claimed speaker role, verified authority, binding intent, and human approval.
sources.limitations: The system must not make a definitive legal authority judgment from call behavior.

sources.title: BGB §310 — Scope of control of standard terms
sources.operationalUse: Capture exact contractual basis and VOB/B version/deviations rather than assuming applicability.
sources.limitations: Not a complete analysis of whether particular VOB/B terms are incorporated or valid.

sources.title: UStG §13b — Recipient as tax debtor
sources.operationalUse: Compare net first and capture supplier-stated tax treatment/evidence for accounting review.
sources.limitations: The agent cannot infer reverse charge solely from a trade label.

sources.title: GWB §1 — Anti-competitive agreements
sources.operationalUse: Prohibit bidder coordination and gate competitor-information disclosure.
sources.limitations: Does not itself answer every buyer-side bid-disclosure scenario.

sources.title: The influence of social motives on integrative negotiation: a meta-analytic review
sources.operationalUse: Combine cooperative problem solving with resistance to unauthorized yielding.
sources.limitations: Experimental tasks are not construction field evidence.

sources.title: Interpersonal trust within negotiations: meta-analytic evidence
sources.operationalUse: Use calibrated trust, transparency, evidence, and correction while retaining verification.
sources.limitations: Does not validate a single rapport script.

sources.title: Communication media and negotiation: two meta-analyses
sources.operationalUse: Treat voice as context-dependent; switch to written/human modes when necessary.
sources.limitations: Older lab studies and heterogeneous tasks.

sources.title: Accuracy of deception judgments
sources.operationalUse: Ban voice/personality lie detection; verify claims from documents and explicit confirmation.
sources.limitations: Human judgment evidence; not a validation of any automated detector.

sources.title: First offers as anchors
sources.operationalUse: Load target and anchor-inconsistent evidence before responding to submitted supplier prices.
sources.limitations: Does not justify invented or overaggressive counter-anchors.

sources.title: The information-anchoring model of first offers
sources.operationalUse: Keep reservation point, budget, urgency, and internal priorities private unless authorized.
sources.limitations: Experimental scenarios; disclosure effects are context-specific.

sources.title: Too precise to pursue: precision and expertise in first offers
sources.operationalUse: Use precise arithmetic but avoid unsupported pseudo-precise negotiated anchors.
sources.limitations: Studied other expert markets.

sources.title: The effect of question phrasing on deception
sources.operationalUse: Ask specific evidence-triggered exclusion questions, then verify against durable evidence.
sources.limitations: Question wording can have relational costs and cannot guarantee truth.

sources.title: Multiple equivalent simultaneous offers reduce the negotiator dilemma
sources.operationalUse: Use at most three mandate-approved commercial packages with a real buyer utility model.
sources.limitations: No technical substitutions; equivalent value must be established, not assumed.

sources.title: Active Listening in Integrative Negotiation
sources.operationalUse: Paraphrase multi-issue answers and ask for correction; do not mirror mechanically.
sources.limitations: Observational German student sample; active listening alone did not improve rapport or understanding.

sources.title: Building and rebuilding trust with promises and apologies
sources.operationalUse: Prohibit bluffing; correct accidental errors immediately and preserve history.
sources.limitations: Experimental trust context.

sources.title: Effects of feigned anger in negotiation
sources.operationalUse: Ban strategic anger and intimidation.
sources.limitations: Does not address genuine emotion.

sources.title: Curtailing deception: direct questions, lies and omissions
sources.operationalUse: Use direct questions but require document reconciliation because questions are not proof.
sources.limitations: Small samples; direct questions can shift the form of deception.

sources.title: Machines versus humans: AI chatbot disclosure and purchases
sources.operationalUse: Keep required AI disclosure concise and state competence/limits immediately.
sources.limitations: Consumer loan-renewal sales, not German B2B construction negotiation.

sources.title: Luca topic distribution
sources.operationalUse: Generate interview questions and field-validation categories only.
sources.limitations: Unknown sample and denominator; ranges overlap and sum inconsistently; must not drive strategy weights or market claims.
